children.heading: Coordinated administration of medicare prescription drug and Medicare Advantage programs
children.label: a Coordinated administration of medicare prescription drug and Medicare Advantage programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services a center to carry out the duties described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: Director
children.label: 2 Director
children.text: Such center shall be headed by a director who shall report directly to the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.label: A
children.text: The administration of parts C and D.

children.label: B
children.text: The provision of notice and information under .section 1395b–2 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Such other duties as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 4 Deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the center is carrying out the duties described in paragraph (3) by not later than .January 1, 2008

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may employ, within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, such individuals as management staff as the Secretary determines to be appropriate. With respect to the administration of parts C and D, such individuals shall include individuals with private sector expertise in negotiations with health benefits plans.

children.label: A
children.text: The review, negotiation, and administration of health care contracts.

children.label: B
children.text: The design of health care benefit plans.

children.label: C
children.text: Actuarial sciences.

children.label: D
children.text: Compliance with health plan contracts.

children.label: E
children.text: Consumer education and decision making.

children.label: F
children.text: Any other area specified by the Secretary that requires specialized management or other expertise.

children.heading: Performance-related pay
children.label: A Performance-related pay
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall establish the rate of pay for an individual employed under paragraph (1). Such rate shall take into account expertise, experience, and performance.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: In no case may the rate of compensation determined under subparagraph (A) exceed the highest rate of basic pay for the Senior Executive Service under .section 5382(b) of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint within the Department of Health and Human Services a Medicare Beneficiary Ombudsman who shall have expertise and experience in the fields of health care and education of (and assistance to) individuals entitled to benefits under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: receive complaints, grievances, and requests for information submitted by individuals entitled to benefits under part A or enrolled under part B, or both, with respect to any aspect of the medicare program;

children.label: i
children.text: assistance in collecting relevant information for such individuals, to seek an appeal of a decision or determination made by a fiscal intermediary, carrier, MA organization, or the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: assistance to such individuals with any problems arising from disenrollment from an MA plan under part C; and

children.label: iii
children.text: assistance to such individuals in presenting information under (relating to income-related premium adjustment; andsection 1395r(i)(4)(C) of this title1So in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the semicolon.1

children.label: C
children.text: submit annual reports to Congress and the Secretary that describe the activities of the Office and that include such recommendations for improvement in the administration of this subchapter as the Ombudsman determines appropriate.

children.heading: Working with health insurance counseling programs
children.label: 3 Working with health insurance counseling programs
children.text: To the extent possible, the Ombudsman shall work with health insurance counseling programs (receiving funding under ) to facilitate the provision of information to individuals entitled to benefits under part A or enrolled under part B, or both regarding MA plans and changes to those plans. Nothing in this paragraph shall preclude further collaboration between the Ombudsman and such programs.section 1395b–4 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: are from entities that manufacture pharmaceutical, biotechnology, medical device, or diagnostic products that are covered or for which coverage is being sought under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: are with respect to coverage, coding, or payment under this subchapter for such products.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: The second sentence of subsection (c)(2) shall apply to the ombudsman under subparagraph (A) in the same manner as such sentence applies to the Medicare Beneficiary Ombudsman under subsection (c).

children.heading: Funding for implementation of beneficiary enrollment simplification
children.label: e Funding for implementation of beneficiary enrollment simplification
children.text: For purposes of carrying out the provisions of and the amendments made by section 120 of division CC of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund under and the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under (in such proportion as the Secretary determines appropriate), to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account, of $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2030, to remain available until expended.section 1395i of this titlesection 1395t of this title
